manifest.description: Comprehensive default seed dataset for global vendor risk management platforms. This is a broad enterprise baseline and not a legal claim of exhaustive coverage of every jurisdiction-specific requirement in the world. Clause/article/control references were added on a curated best-fit basis for selected major frameworks where exact identifiers are stable and broadly used in audit reporting. This version adds a normalized universal control layer and distinct compliance framework clause entities so exact clause reporting can be generated without forcing every operational control to map directly to every framework requirement.

exact_reference_enriched_frameworks.framework_version: AICPA 2017 Trust Services Criteria (2022 revised points of focus)
45 CFR Part 164 (HIPAA Security Rule and selected Privacy Rule provisions)

clause_reference_coverage.note: Long-tail frameworks remain framework-level only in this file to avoid fabricating clause-level precision where no curated exact reference was added.

normalization_coverage.note: Exact clause/article/control reporting should use universal_control_clause_mappings. Long-tail frameworks that remain unmapped at exact reference level continue to flow through universal_control_framework_mappings as a framework-level fallback.

vendor_risk_frameworks.purpose: Assess continuity planning, BIA, dependency mapping, and test maturity.

risk_tiers.name: Tier 1 - Critical Vendor
risk_tiers.description: Direct customer, revenue, safety, or regulatory impact; high dependency or privileged access.

risk_tiers.name: Tier 2 - High Risk Vendor
risk_tiers.description: Processes sensitive data or supports important operations with meaningful dependency.

risk_tiers.name: Tier 3 - Medium Risk Vendor
risk_tiers.description: Moderate operational dependency or limited confidential data access.

risk_tiers.name: Tier 4 - Low Risk Vendor
risk_tiers.description: Low dependency, limited or no system/data access, low regulatory impact.
